data.title: The address that hoarded 11.5 million OM tokens 11 months ago transferred all the tokens to Binance, and the sale will make a profit of US$77 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Spot On Chain monitoring, the smart money address "0x177e6" earned $77 million by trading OM in less than a year. The address hoarded 11.5 million OM through Binance in March 2024, with an average price of $0.63 and a cost of $6.9 million.
Today, OM rose to $7.53. The address began to transfer these tokens to Binance through multiple addresses in the past 24 hours. If all are sold, it will make a profit of $84.5 million, with a profit of $77 million.

data.title: Data: Bitcoin's average transaction fee drops to lowest level in recent years
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn wrote that the average transaction fee for Bitcoin is currently 0.000013 BTC (about $1.27), reaching its lowest point in many years. Bitcoin transaction fees have soared several times in May 2023, December 2023, and April 2024. These increases were mainly driven by the launch of the Runes protocol and the sharp rise in Bitcoin prices. At that time, the number of unconfirmed transactions in the mempool (transaction memory pool) increased significantly, causing users to pay higher fees for faster confirmation. Today, the mempool is idle, the popularity of Bitcoin Inscriptions has declined, and many retail investors seem to have exited the market again. Now is a great time to optimize wallet management. If your Bitcoin is scattered across multiple addresses, now is a low-cost opportunity to merge them.

data.title: Bitcoin's Inter-Exchange Flow Pulse Turns Bearish
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 15, CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn reported that the Bitcoin Inter-Exchange Flow Pulse (IFP) indicator has turned bearish. This indicator utilizes CryptoQuant's data on Bitcoin exchange flows to assess the movement of funds between spot and derivatives trading platforms. The flow data reflects market sentiment:

- An increase in Bitcoin inflow to derivatives platforms is typically associated with bullish market conditions.

- A decrease in Bitcoin outflow from derivatives platforms is usually linked to bearish market conditions.

data.title: Bitcoin Transaction Fees Reach Multi-Year Low
data.description: According to PANews, CryptoQuant analyst Maartunn reports that Bitcoin's average transaction fee has dropped to 0.000013 BTC, approximately $1.27, marking a multi-year low. In May 2023, December 2023, and April 2024, Bitcoin transaction fees surged significantly, driven by the launch of the Runes protocol and substantial increases in Bitcoin's price. During these periods, the number of unconfirmed transactions in the mempool rose sharply, prompting users to pay higher fees for faster confirmations. Currently, the mempool is idle, and the popularity of Bitcoin inscriptions has waned. This situation presents a low-cost opportunity for consolidating funds if Bitcoin is distributed across multiple addresses.

data.title: Key Economic Events To Watch In The Upcoming Week
data.description:
According to PANews, the recent CPI report indicates a rise in U.S. consumer inflation, prompting U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to acknowledge the need for further efforts to alleviate price pressures in the economy. This has dampened expectations for interest rate cuts, although weak retail sales data has provided some hope. Wall Street's ability to handle dramatic events has been tested once again, with traders proving capable, leaving the overall market largely unaffected. Here are the key points the market will focus on in the upcoming week:

On Monday at 22:30 (UTC+8), Philadelphia Fed President Patrick Harker, a 2026 FOMC voting member, will speak. At 23:20 (UTC+8), Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman will deliver remarks.

On Tuesday at 23:20 (UTC+8), San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly, a 2027 FOMC voting member, will address the public.

On Thursday at 03:00 (UTC+8), the Federal Reserve will release the minutes of its January monetary policy meeting. At 21:30 (UTC+8), data on initial jobless claims for the week ending February 15 and the February Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index will be published. At 22:35 (UTC+8), Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, a 2025 FOMC voting member, will speak.

On Friday at 01:05 (UTC+8), St. Louis Fed President James Bullard, a 2025 FOMC voting member, will speak at the New York Economic Club. At 22:45 (UTC+8), the preliminary February S&P Global Manufacturing PMI and Services PMI will be released. At 23:00 (UTC+8), the final February University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index and the final February one-year inflation rate expectations will be announced.

Following Powell's semi-annual testimony and January's inflation data, investors are unlikely to focus heavily on the Fed's January meeting minutes next week. Instead, attention may shift to Friday's February S&P Global PMI data. A PMI below 50 could pressure the dollar and boost gold prices. A February survey by Bank of America of over 50 global fund managers revealed that betting on a stronger dollar remains a crowded trade among interest rate and currency traders. Nearly half of investors still expect the dollar to peak in the first quarter of this year.

data.title: XRP Faces Potential Short-Term Pullback After Recent Surge
data.description: According to Odaily, blockchain analyst Ali has indicated on X that XRP might experience a brief pullback. This comes after a 22% increase in recent days, with the TD Sequential indicator showing a sell signal on XRP's 4-hour chart.

data.title: TD Sequential indicator shows sell on XRP 4-hour chart, may pull back
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: On-chain analyst Ali Yu X wrote that XRP may experience a short-term correction. After rising 22% in recent days, the TD Sequential indicator showed a sell signal on the XRP 4-hour chart.

data.title: Solana Apps Generate 10x More Revenue Than Ethereum: Research
data.description:
Solana doubled Ethereum’s trading volume, hitting $5.39B vs. $2.14B, while perpetual trading reached $1.09B, surpassing Ethereum’s $470M.
source: https://cryptopotato.com/solana-apps-generate-10x-more-revenue-than-ethereum-research/
